title: geonoma multiflora subsp. hexasticha
intro: Geonoma multiflora subsp. hexasticha, also known as the six-branched Geonoma palm, is a type of tropical plant native to Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. The leaves of the Geonoma multiflora subsp. hexasticha are used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's extracts are used in traditional medicine to prepare ointments and creams for skin conditions and wounds.
